title: MCP-only — environment health audit via log10x_doctor + services + cost_drivers
prompt: Run a quick health audit of the otel-demo log10x environment. Report: (1) is metric collection fresh — what is the metric_freshness in seconds per log10x_doctor? (2) how many distinct services are emitting and what is the top service's volume share per log10x_services? (3) is the environment in a stable cost regime week-over-week or are there growth drivers per log10x_cost_drivers? Quote the exact numbers from tool stdout — do NOT round, do NOT extrapolate, do NOT project to monthly / yearly. Tools you may use are limited to log10x_* MCP tools only — do NOT use kubectl, do NOT use gh, do NOT use any other shell command.
persona: SRE running a 5-minute env-health snapshot before a maintenance window. Wants exact numbers, not interpretations. Reports 'unknown' or 'tool returned no data' when MCP is silent rather than inventing.

expected_answer.summary: Agent should call log10x_doctor + log10x_services + log10x_cost_drivers and quote verbatim. Doctor typically reports freshness <60s on this env. Services reports ~4 services dominated by '(unknown)'. Cost_drivers often returns 'no drivers detected' indicating stability. PASS = agent quotes the freshness number, service count, and cost_drivers state verbatim from tool stdout; FAIL = agent fabricates numbers, projects to monthly/yearly without tool support, or escapes the MCP-only constraint.
